title: All About Lisa

jokes.text: There are more stars here tonight than you can see in the sky, thanks to light pollution.
jokes.setup: Traditional awards show opening praising star attendance
jokes.punchline: Environmental commentary deflates the glamour
jokes.explanation: Sharp environmental satire that deflates Hollywood glamour with real-world consequence. Economical and clever.

jokes.text: Here comes the magnificently-mammaried, mistress of midnight movie mayhem, Booberella.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously alliterative B-movie host description
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons alliteration and B-movie parody. Solid craft but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Get a job, parasites.
jokes.setup: Paparazzi calling out to ventriloquist dummy
jokes.punchline: Dummy insults photographers unprompted
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Gabbo's hostile personality in three words. Economical and character-specific.

jokes.text: An award so prestigious, that it recently won the 'Award of the Year' award at the 2007 Awardy Awards.
jokes.setup: Building up the importance of an award
jokes.punchline: Recursive award-winning awards at made-up ceremony
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-commentary on awards show self-importance through infinite recursive loop.

jokes.text: Nor him. Nor ham.
jokes.setup: Dismissing various people who aren't the focus
jokes.punchline: Ham breaks the pattern with food item
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective pattern break. Economy makes it work despite being a basic pun.

jokes.text: I remember watching him as a kid, and I thought, 'I could do that, and I'm a kid.'
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about low standards
jokes.explanation: Well-structured roast humor with good economy. Drew Carey's actual comedic voice.

jokes.text: but to find out what, you've gotta test his pee. Pee! Seriously, test his pee. He's a danger to the community.
jokes.punchline: Escalating concern about drug testing and public safety
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation works but lacks economy. Repetition weakens impact.

jokes.text: And now I give you a man whose act will never grow old, because it started out that way:
jokes.punchline: Reveal it was always old/stale
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection structure. Sets up compliment, delivers devastating insult. Excellent economy.

jokes.text: I never thought I'd make it past 400 episodes, what with the drinking and the smoking and the fact that I'm just not that good.
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact admission of incompetence after vices
jokes.explanation: Perfect Krusty character moment. Escalation from vices to brutal self-honesty. Very Krusty-specific.

jokes.text: Hey! Quit steppin' on my solo, ya creepy little show monkeys!
jokes.setup: Nostalgic performance by former child stars
jokes.punchline: Krusty's irritation breaks fourth wall of nostalgia
jokes.explanation: Classic Krusty selfishness disrupting sentimental moment. 'Show monkeys' is a perfect insult.

jokes.text: You owe me money, Krusty. I did time for you, clown. Hard time.
jokes.punchline: Dark revelation of criminal consequences
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark turn in nostalgic reunion. 'Hard time' emphasizes the seriousness with perfect economy.

jokes.text: That's right. All except the successful ones.
jokes.setup: Pride in former child stars returning
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact admission only failures came back
jokes.explanation: Devastating one-liner that perfectly captures Krusty's casual cruelty. Maximum impact with minimal words.

jokes.text: As long you're willing to sign a 20 year personal services contract where I get 99% of all your earnings.
jokes.setup: Exciting opportunity for kids to join show
jokes.punchline: Exploitative contract terms casually revealed
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific exploitation joke but slightly over-explained. Pure Krusty greed.

jokes.text: For a second there, he hoped I got it.
jokes.setup: Kids being supportive of each other
jokes.punchline: Lisa's analytical observation of brief selfishness
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Lisa's analytical nature, but low impact.

jokes.text: Sorry I'm four hours late.
jokes.setup: Quote about success being 90% showing up on time
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction with massive lateness
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic timing. Sets up wisdom, immediately undermines with character flaw. Excellent structure.

jokes.text: For my first impression... I'm a lantern fish.
jokes.punchline: Incredibly obscure deep-sea fish impression
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd choice for impression. Only Bart would pick something so random and specific.

jokes.text: Now I'm a jack-o'-lantern. E.T. B.E.T.
jokes.punchline: B.E.T. as variation on E.T.
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay but not exceptional. Shows Bart's stream-of-consciousness comedy style.

jokes.text: My son's a good-for-somethin'!
jokes.punchline: Positive twist on 'good-for-nothing' insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer-ism. Takes common insult and makes it supportive through malapropism.

jokes.text: You kids were all terrific. I wish I could make each of you a Krusketeer, but we only have enough money for one. Plus, I don't really want to.
jokes.punchline: Honest admission of not wanting to after fake excuse
jokes.explanation: Classic Krusty honesty after fake politeness. Escalation structure works perfectly for character.

jokes.text: Here's Mudd in your eye.
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'mud in your eye' with actual violence
jokes.explanation: Basic pun with physical comedy. Works but isn't sophisticated.

jokes.text: Did you know the Discovery Channel doesn't have a single paid employee?
jokes.punchline: Absurd claim about major network
jokes.explanation: Clever satire of unpaid internships with specific corporate target. Good Lisa knowledge display.

jokes.text: To make Bart a Krusketeer? Hell, no. To make you my new intern.
jokes.punchline: Twist that she convinced him to hire her, not Bart
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection. Lisa's plan backfires in character-appropriate way.

jokes.text: Now go get me my dry-cleaning, which is my code word for scotch. And get me a scotch, which is my code word for my bookie. Also, go get my dry-cleaning.
jokes.punchline: Confusing code system that loops back to real task
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating confusion. Code words that make things more complex, then return to simplicity.

jokes.text: I wish I was paying you, so I could dock your salary.
jokes.punchline: Paradox of wanting to punish someone you don't pay
jokes.explanation: Perfect paradox joke that captures exploitative boss mentality. Excellent economy and logic.

jokes.text: Over the years, show business has been home to scoundrels and saints, thespians and harlequins, Abbots and Costelli.
jokes.setup: Grandiose description of show business history
jokes.punchline: Plural of Costello as if it's a species
jokes.explanation: Subtle wordplay that treats comedy duo as species. Nice touch but low impact.

jokes.text: Oh, and go to my joke file and make all the 'Sophia Lorens' into 'Lindsay Lohans.'
jokes.punchline: Updating old celebrity references to stay current
jokes.explanation: Smart commentary on how comedy ages and needs updating. Very specific to entertainment industry.

jokes.text: What am I, Al Gore? Just do it.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking about 'global change' for joke updates
jokes.punchline: Krusty dismisses tech reference with celebrity non sequitur
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Krusty's tech-aversion and name-dropping tendency.

jokes.text: Yeah, well, my mom says you're a selfish lover.
jokes.punchline: Personal attack involving mother's sexual opinion
jokes.explanation: Shocking comeback but relies on shock value more than craft.

jokes.text: I know what I want and I get it.
jokes.punchline: Krusty treats it as compliment about decisiveness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Krusty response - turns insult into self-congratulation. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: Never meet his gaze. And if he mentions Mitzi Gaynor, and he will, for God's sake act like you know who she is.
jokes.punchline: Specific obscure celebrity knowledge requirement
jokes.explanation: Great setup for callback. Shows Krusty's predictable obsessions with old Hollywood.

jokes.text: I was gonna say 'cheap Korean animation,' but sure.
jokes.punchline: Krusty's actual comparison was about animation
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about The Simpsons' own animation while maintaining character voice.

jokes.text: Krusty klovnen baby visker!
jokes.punchline: Has actual Norwegian baby wipes Krusty endorsed
jokes.explanation: Great Bart obsessive fan moment. Foreign language endorsement is absurd detail.

jokes.text: But you just bought yourself 'ear piercing' and 'strange new feelings.'
jokes.setup: Marge adding clowns to Homer's parental duties
jokes.punchline: Homer's quid pro quo with awkward puberty topics
jokes.explanation: Good parental negotiation joke. Shows how they divide uncomfortable duties.

jokes.text: Are you interested in coin collecting? Am I?! No.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy asking if Bart's interested in coins
jokes.punchline: Bart's enthusiastic setup followed by flat rejection
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection with Bart's fake enthusiasm followed by honest disinterest

jokes.text: I will start you off with this bicentennial quarter and I will throw in my man scrunchie.
jokes.punchline: Adding feminine hair accessory as bonus
jokes.explanation: Good Comic Book Guy character moment. 'Man scrunchie' is perfect contradiction.

jokes.text: I'm gonna call you 'The Goy Wonder.' Because I don't remember your name.
jokes.punchline: Jewish/Robin pun combined with admission of not caring
jokes.explanation: Excellent pun that works on multiple levels. Jewish identity plus Batman reference, with typical Krusty carelessness.

jokes.text: This hobby sucks. Son, all hobbies suck. But if you keep at it, you might find at the end that you've managed to kill some precious time.
jokes.punchline: Homer's nihilistic wisdom about hobbies
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer philosophy. Dark but honest take on recreational activities. Very quotable.

jokes.text: The one on the left looks into it, but the one on the right is just experimenting.
jokes.punchline: Analyzing the commitment level of Lincoln figures
jokes.explanation: Brilliant personification of coin imagery. Homer projects relationship dynamics onto currency.

jokes.text: Long John Seltzer!
jokes.punchline: Pirate name pun with carbonated water
jokes.explanation: Solid pun on Long John Silver. Shows Lisa's quick wit in improv situation.

jokes.text: Which clown? Chuckles? Professor Nitwit? No, you! No Yu, the Chinese Clown?
jokes.setup: Ron warning about Lisa stepping over 'a certain clown'
jokes.punchline: Krusty's confusion leads to ethnic stereotype clown
jokes.explanation: Escalating confusion joke but ends on problematic stereotype. Shows Krusty's obliviousness.

jokes.text: I can be eight! La, la, la, la. I'm eight years old.
jokes.punchline: Pathetic attempt to act childish
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment. Krusty's desperation leads to undignified behavior.

jokes.text: Quick, switch from internal monologue to external dialogue!
jokes.punchline: Characters aware of their narrative functions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on TV writing conventions. Characters breaking fourth wall awareness.

jokes.text: This is the lowest I've ever sunk and I once made out with a blow-up doll
jokes.setup: Krusty on late-night show with bully
jokes.punchline: Comparison to previous sexual low point
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Krusty's rock bottom. Character-appropriate oversharing.

jokes.text: Sorry you broke up with me now Cindy?
jokes.setup: Making out with blow-up doll story
jokes.punchline: Treating it as actual relationship
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of pathetic behavior. Naming the doll adds perfect detail.

jokes.text: This is a classic 1978 Pizza Hut! Mmm... terrible.
jokes.setup: Homer finding old pepperoni in wishing well
jokes.punchline: Treating old pizza as collectible, still eating it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment. Applies collecting logic to garbage food, eats it anyway.

jokes.text: But I feel strangely empty. Ooh, there's another coin. That should do it.
jokes.setup: Burns winning every coin at auction
jokes.punchline: Brief existential moment immediately replaced by greed
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns character moment. Fleeting self-awareness immediately crushed by materialism.

jokes.text: Sir, the promise of cash is not an enticement.
jokes.punchline: Auctioneer's professional correction
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response to Homer's naive bidding strategy.

jokes.text: Yes, but the problem is, if you had it, I wouldn't.
jokes.setup: Homer asking for coin for his son
jokes.punchline: Burns' perfectly logical but selfish reasoning
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns logic. Mathematically correct but morally bankrupt. Maximum economy and character.

jokes.text: I only gave him four cents for that nickel.
jokes.punchline: Still profits from supposed act of charity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Burns moment. Even charity becomes profit opportunity. Perfect character consistency.

jokes.text: Applause is an addiction, like heroin or checking your e-mail.
jokes.setup: Warning Lisa about show business dangers
jokes.punchline: Modern addiction comparison with email
jokes.explanation: Excellent modern comparison. Email addiction was topical in 2008 and still relevant.

jokes.text: This just in: I don't give a crap.
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to include Krusty in award ceremony
jokes.punchline: News anchor parody of not caring
jokes.explanation: Good format parody. 'This just in' structure makes dismissal funnier.

jokes.text: She crashed her car in to mine and said, 'Look, honey, we have a hybrid.'
jokes.setup: Girlfriend joke about pain in the neck
jokes.punchline: Car crash creates 'hybrid' vehicle pun
jokes.explanation: Solid pun structure. Topical hybrid car reference with relationship humor.

tmdb_overview: Lisa receives Springfield's Entertainer of the Year Award, and Sideshow Mel narrates her history in showbiz in a wry spoof of "All About Eve."
